methodsAttempted.query: site:ucdavis.edu UC Davis generative AI policy students academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Found UC Davis IET guidance, an IET student guide, UC Davis Health guidance, a School of Medicine policy, library guide resources, a UC Davis Magazine article, and course or unit pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucdavis.edu "generative AI" "UC Davis" teaching learning policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Found Center for Educational Effectiveness teaching guidance and UC Davis Library links to campus AI resources.
Found the Aggie AI tools index and Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat product brief.
Followed official Aggie AI links from AI Guidance to the student guide, AI tools, and Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat pages.
Followed public UC Davis Health AI guidance and School of Medicine policy references; login-gated SharePoint and Ellucid policy links were rejected.

artifacts.summary: Official UC Davis AI policy and guidance evidence was found primarily on UC Davis IET Aggie AI pages, the Center for Educational Effectiveness, UC Davis Health Compliance and Privacy Services, and UC Davis Health School of Medicine policy pages. News, division-specific, and login-gated official sources were documented but not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.claimText: UC Davis guidance tells UC Davis and UC Davis Health community members to use UC Davis-approved AI platforms, or UC Davis-developed tools, for university work.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Use UC Davis-approved or UC Davis-developed AI tools for university work.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UC Davis guidance says AI tools used for Administration and Services responsibilities require committee review and approval before campus or health departments proceed with procurement.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Administration and Services AI tools require review and approval before departmental procurement.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UC Davis guidance says users should not enter sensitive UC Davis data, including student, personnel, patient, confidential research, or financial account information, into AI tools.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Do not enter sensitive UC Davis data into AI tools.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UC Davis guidance tells students to confirm with instructors before using AI in coursework and not to use AI to complete assignments unless explicitly permitted.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Students should confirm instructor permission before coursework AI use.

artifacts.normalizedValue: CEE advises explicit course AI policies and clear student communication.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is limited to UC Davis guidance for approved or university-developed AI tools and uses direct IET evidence.
Approved because the claim is narrowed to Administration and Services AI procurement review, matching the IET source scope.
Approved because the sensitive-data categories are copied from the UC Davis IET guidance and the claim does not extend beyond AI tools.
Approved because the claim tracks the student-facing instruction to confirm with instructors and not complete assignments with AI unless explicitly permitted.
Approved as a narrow source-status and approved-tools claim about the existence of an Aggie AI tools list rather than a claim that every listed tool is available to all users.
Approved because the Copilot claim combines availability with the page's security limitation and does not generalize to all AI tools.
Approved because the claim is scoped to Center for Educational Effectiveness teaching guidance, not a binding university-wide rule.
Approved because the claim is explicitly scoped to UC Davis Health and patient or sensitive data.
Approved because the claim is limited to UC Davis School of Medicine medical students and follows the policy wording on written permission.
Approved because the claim is limited to the School of Medicine policy's patient documentation and PHI restrictions.

artifacts.title: University of California, Davis AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review for University of California, Davis found official UC Davis and UC Davis Health guidance on approved AI tools, procurement review, sensitive data, student coursework expectations, teaching guidance, and UC Davis Health and School of Medicine AI restrictions. Claims remain staged candidates and are not canonical public conclusions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
UC Davis Health and School of Medicine claims are scoped to those units and should not be generalized to all UC Davis coursework.
Login-gated UC Davis Health policy documents were not accessed and are represented only as rejected or inaccessible sources.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
